entry.title: Re: Urgent how DOCX(Xstring) is converted to PDF(Xstring)
entry.content:
I found this function module : EXT_CONVERT_DOCX_TO_PDF
pass your i_docx_xstring to get lv_pdf_xstring, should work

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Connection
entry.content:
Dpagent vs Cloud conector.

SAP DPAgent (Data Provisioning Agent) and SAP Cloud Connector are both used to facilitate secure communication between on-premise systems and SAP cloud environments, but they serve different purposes and are used in different contexts.

SAP DPAgent (Data Provisioning Agent)
- Purpose. SAP DPAgent is primarily used for data replication and real-time data access between on-premise systems and SAP cloud solutions like SAP HANA Cloud or SAP Data Intelligence.
- Functionality: It enables data provisioning by connecting on-premise data sources (such as SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, or other databases) to the cloud. It supports real-time data replication and can be used for scenarios like data federation, where data is accessed in real-time without being physically moved.
- Use Cases**: Commonly used in scenarios requiring real-time data integration, such as SAP HANA Smart Data Integration (SDI) or SAP Data Intelligence.

SAP Cloud Connector
- Purpose**: SAP Cloud Connector acts as a secure link between on-premise systems and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) or other SAP cloud applications like SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP SuccessFactors.
- Functionality**: It provides a secure tunnel for communication, ensuring that only authorized cloud applications can access on-premise systems. It handles authentication, encryption, and connectivity, enabling seamless integration between on-premise and cloud environments.
- Use Cases**: Used for integrating on-premise systems with SAP cloud applications, such as SAP BTP, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, or SAP SuccessFactors. It is also used in scenarios like SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) for connecting to on-premise systems.

 Key Differences
1. **Primary Use**:
- DPAgent: Focuses on data replication and real-time data access.
- Cloud Connector: Focuses on secure connectivity and integration between on-premise and cloud applications.

2. ntegration Scenarios**:
- DPAgent: Used for data provisioning and real-time data integration.
- Cloud Connector: Used for application integration and secure communication.

3. Supported Systems**:
- DPAgent: Connects to on-premise data sources for data replication.
- Cloud Connector: Connects to on-premise systems for application-level integration.

4. Deployment Context**:
- DPAgent: Typically used in data-centric scenarios like SAP HANA Cloud or SAP Data Intelligence.
- Cloud Connector: Used in application-centric scenarios like SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

In summary, SAP DPAgent is more focused on data integration and replication, while SAP Cloud Connector is designed for secure application integration between on-premise and cloud environments.

entry.title: Re: Joule – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach
entry.content:
Hi, @ebsor ebsor 

Check the customizing tenant isn't register.  

then you have unregister the customizing tenant and back to register for get token.

entry.title: Hi Experts i am trying to manipulate shelf life expiration date from badi VB_SLED_MANAGEMENT
entry.content:
But when i am putting debugged IF_EX_VB_SLED_MANAGEMENT~MAINTAIN_SLED_PARAMETERS but its not triggering from MSC1/2/3n or from migo and when i do a where used list on VB_SLED_MANAGEMENT i am not getting any result in hana system. Can someone please help me to understand how to trigger debugging for this it will be of great help.

As i see this badi is there in the official sap help document for sled determnation for batch.

entry.title: Sydney Stammtisch Recap 2502
entry.content:
The Shelbourne Hotel Rooftop was another new venue for #SAPStammtischSYD and we had big crowd come to check it out. Thanks to everyone who came!

It was a sunny day but very humid (as it usually is in Sydney in February). At first I was disappointed that our tables weren't directly under the retractable roof, but then I realised that our space was air conditioned and much more clement.

I couldn't really count numbers precisely but I would estimate we had a record crowd, probably over 40 people.

I'm thinking we will need to plan further ahead for future events, as we really need a large space reserved for us.  Most likely the next event will be on a Thursday in May.

Perhaps we should return to The Orient Hotel? Thursday seems to be the most popular night...would you agree?  Or is there a new venue we should try?  Let us know in the comments below

Thanks to @jeff_t for the last 6 photos below. Feel free to add photos in the comments

entry.title: SAP BTP Trial invitation e-mails
entry.content:
I recently signed up for a BTP trial.

When I try to add users (under sub-account -> Security -> Users) none of them get an invitation email. There are no errors or warnings. I tried to see if associating the users with a Role Collection has any impact, but it doesn't.

Since this is a basic functionality I wonder if I am missing some basic config somewhere.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Vendor field is not getting the value at the time of...
entry.content: I have worked with SAP to find the solution. At this level based on your screenshot i can say, please check the Partitioned data of the supplier in Ariba Buying&Invoicing. The vendor doesn't populate in these documents if Partitioned data is missing.

entry.title: Re: How to download/extract custom Fiori app source code...
entry.content:
Besides the Tcode /UI5/UI5_REPOSITORY_LOAD
You can also use ABAPGit:
If you have ABAPGit installed, you can use it to export the source code to a Git repository
Hope this helps
Cheers!

please use Best practices explorer  : Process Navigator - SAP for Me
more

entry.title: Re: Hi Experts i am trying to manipulate shelf life expir...
entry.content:
Hey, 

normally it sits in  Programm: SAPMM07M. 

But if debugger wont open it could you check if its the correct one for you? 

Maybe try to check if you are running this badi with your user or you might have to debugg for an external user. 

If you are triggering the programm with an message you have to debugg with RSNAST00. 
If its done via Job you could try /jdbg  for planned job to debug from there. 
Hope that helps

entry.title: BOMMAT: how to control if the previous idoc is not successfully , next idoc run will be error
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are using BOMMAT to migrate the ECN BOM from ECC to S4.  we are using sterilization no to process the idoc of same material + plant + Usage+ alternative no. 

the problem is : now system behavior is ， even the second IDoc of the bom processing is error. but the next idoc no. is still running with successful status 53.  this will cause the bom data is inconsistent . 

Any standard configuration or control set can archive my requirement? 

#BOM

thank you all.

entry.title: Re: BOMMAT: how to control if the previous idoc is not successfully , next idoc run will be error
entry.content: serialization no. can only control the processing sequence. but cannot block the next idoc processing , it is not dependent on previous idoc status

entry.title: Re: How to Implement Production in Default SAP Business one
entry.content:
Detailed Approach!

I am facing one problem in receipt of production; i want experts help to sort out the issue i will share screenshots 

i suppose to receive only 395 pcs, but i added mistakenly quantity was 1210 pcs

for 1210 pcs 2 raw material required

1st raw material required - 404 pcs but only 294.5 pcs stock
2nd raw material required - 15 pcs

so difference is posted in variance in summary tab i dont know how to adjust it.

entry.title: Re: Hi Experts i am trying to manipulate shelf life expir...
entry.content: Check OMJ5 if it is active for your Plant or moving type

entry.title: Re: Hi Experts i am trying to manipulate shelf life expiration date from badi VB_SLED_MANAGEMENT
entry.content:
Did you make sure that your BAdI implementation and class are both activated? Did you make sure that you're in the conditions to call this BAdI? NB: the where-used list provided by SAP is well known to be incomplete since ABAP 6.10 - R/3 4.70, as explained in https://me.sap.com/notes/401389.

EDIT: if you still can't debug it, as said in the answer, you may reverse engineer SAPMM07M to see which subroutines are calling the BAdI and debug them to identify which one of the conditions are not fulfilled.

entry.title: DWS class grouping
entry.content:
The "hourly" and "salaried" employees of a manufacturing unit which share the same planned working times (9 to 6) have only one difference - overtime calculation rule (5% and 10%)

1) In this case, do we have two Daily work schedules and assign two different DWS classes?

OR

2) do we create a single DWS with a single DWS class and handle this scenario in PCRs?

@vianshu @sikindar_a

entry.title: Re: How to remove the header that automatically added to...
entry.content:
You can use the URL Parameter:

sap-ushell-config=headerless

But I think you try to build a navigation inside your app. I would suggest to decompose your app and let the launchpad take over the navigation part.

entry.title: what is the released badi for warehouse order sorting in the public cloud system

entry.title: Guide to Mass Uploading Product Master in EWM using Warehouse Monitor & /SCWM/MIG_PRODUCT
entry.content:
In this document, I will explain how to mass upload the product master in the SAP EWM system within S4HANA using the warehouse monitor and /SCWM/MIG_PRODUCT.

When you create a material using the MM01 transaction, your master data will automatically transfer to the EWM system due to the embedded connection.

For example, if you create the material MAT200 in MM01, you can see the material data created in ERP tables such as MARA, MARD, and MARC.However, if you attempt to view the same data in the T code /SCWM/MAT1(warehouse level), you will encounter an error. This occurs because the material created in MM01 means that the global data of the material has been established, making it accessible for all SAP modules such as EWM, TM, MDG, etc. This is a functionality of the embedded system.In the transaction code /SAPAPO/MAT1, you can view all basic global-level data created in the ERP system.Now, we will focus on extending product data with the warehouse view in the EWM system.

To accomplish this, we will use the transaction code /SCWM/MAT1, which allows us to extend both the warehouse view of the product and specific storage types.

It's important to note the following:

- Your warehouse view data will be stored in the database table /SAPAPO/MATLWH ( not a product as a direct entry, pass MATNR to /SAPAPO/MATKEY and get the MATID then pass here).

- Data related to warehouse storage types will be stored in the database table /SAPAPO/MATLWHST.

Here is an example of this,

I have maintained the warehouse data but not the storage-type data. Now let's see how it appears on the tables.Mass upload product master in S4 HANA EWM:

Mass uploading of the product master in EWM means extending the warehouse view/storage type view for materials that have already been created in MM01 within ERP. Therefore, it is essential that the material master is created prior to performing the mass extension of the product master.

I have created the following material in MM01 (in ERP), and there is no warehouse data maintained yet.We can check the list of products that do not have maintained warehouse data in the warehouse monitor belowMass upload/extend using -/SCWM/MIG_PRODUCT:

Refer the SAP KBA – “2602348 - Tools for Batch Warehouse Product Maintenance in S/4Hana embedded EWM”

In the KBA above, SAP explained how to use /SCWM/MIG_PRODUCT, and a sample file is also attached to the note. We can download the file named "scwm_mig_product_template_NEW.xlsx" and fill in our product master details there.

Below is the screenshot of the filled data from the file, and I converted this file to a .CSV format when saving it on the local drive.Execute /SCWM/MIG_PRODUCTAfter executing, we are getting the below error,The error occurs because we have not selected a field separator on the selection screen. If the field separator is not chosen, the system will not be able to read the spreadsheet data correctly. Therefore, ensure that the field separator is set to a comma (,) on the initial screen.

Now, let's fill in the fields and execute the process again.After executing the process again, we can now see that everything is green and there are no errors.We can check the extended product details in the warehouse monitor as below,Product Master extension using warehouse monitor:

Additionally, we can extend the product master using the warehouse monitor, and below we will test that functionality with the test case.

Please refer SAP KBA-“2627825 - Mass Creation of Warehouse Products from Monitor” & “2602348 - Tools for Batch Warehouse Product Maintenance in S/4Hana embedded EWM”In the screenshot below, we can see that the product is created in MM01, but the warehouse data is not extended.Follow the steps below to extend it,We have filled in the following details, which will apply to all the items selected on the previous screen. However, please note that this means all the selected products will have the same data entered here. If you need different data for specific items, those materials must be processed separately.The warehouse data for the materials has now been created.In the same way, we can maintain the storage type data and follow the screenshots for extending the storage type data view for the products.Enter the storage type where you will extend the product in the selection screen,Now, storage-type data is also maintained for the product.

Note:

We can use the warehouse monitor to make mass changes to warehouse products. If a product is already extended, the system will apply the changes based on the data provided. However, if the product is not extended in EWM, the monitor will create/extend the product according to the given data.

Ref:

2627825 - Mass Creation of Warehouse Products from Monitor

2602348 - Tools for Batch Warehouse Product Maintenance in S/4Hana embedded EWM

All data and screenshots in this documentation originate from the SAP S/4HANA 2023 demo system. This system is configured for training, testing, and demonstration purposes only and is not connected to any production environment. Any similarity to actual business data, entities, or individuals is purely coincidental.

Best Regards,

Kannan Chokkanathan

entry.title: Re: Block leave counter solution in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:

Wanted to understand if this configuration is meant to decide whether the leave requested falls under the category of BlockLeave.

Or is there something more than that.

Thanks

Pradeep

entry.title: Refresh Object Page after Action Execution with Custom Entity
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am using the RAP Model with a Custom Entity and have implemented an Unmanaged Behavior Definition. I want to refresh the Object Page after executing an action.

However, since the Custom Entity does not have a projection, I cannot use the "use side effects" syntax in the projection. I have tried using "side effects affect ..." but the Object Page does not refresh.

Is there any way to refresh the Object Page when using a Custom Entity in RAP?

Thank a lot.

entry.title: Sap analytics cloud - deleting version data using data action
entry.content:
hi all,

i need to allow my planning users to delete records from the planning budget version, this sometimes a neccessity because the budget changes and new budget is being uploaded,

i created a data action using the advanced forumla editor and i came across a problem, i'm unable to select a version to run this data action on, it's not even showing in the filters on the visual editor. What can i do?

entry.title: 🚀 Aspiring SAP Professional | SAP Ariba & S/4HANA MM Certified | Open to Opportunities & Guidance
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

I am an aspiring SAP professional actively seeking job opportunities within the SAP ecosystem. As a graduate of the SAP Young Professional Program (YPP) in the UK, I have gained hands-on experience in SAP S/4HANA Sourcing & Procurement (MM) and SAP Ariba.

I am SAP Certified in Sourcing & Procurement (TS452-2022) and Business Process Integration with SAP S/4HANA (TS410), with expertise in procurement processes, SAP MM customization, SAP Ariba, stakeholder management, and process optimization.

With over two years of experience in procurement and supply chain across industries, I have worked on SAP implementations, vendor management, contract negotiations, and data management. I am open to junior or support roles in SAP and am willing to relocate with no location preference.

Currently, I am based in London, UK and would love to connect with fellow SAP professionals for guidance, mentorship, and potential opportunities. Please feel free to reach out or share any leads!

📩 Email: madhusudankhedekar@gmail.com

Looking forward to being part of the SAP community and contributing to exciting projects! 🚀

entry.title: Re: Configuration of configurable product is empty on subscription order and contract.
entry.content: Is this already resolved? Any hints, please? We are in a similar situation @Rafafellt

entry.title: Re: Urgent how DOCX(Xstring) is converted to PDF(Xstring)
entry.content: I used different solutions, the ways these questions with answer marked "solved" say how to do it.

entry.title: Re: We need text value like more or less, but instead of...
entry.content:
@Vishwajeet-Singh 

SAC cannot store textual data into measures. It can only store numeric values. One possible work around could be that you store numeric values for each of those texts like below and then use thresholds to color code them and only show threshold icons. 

2- More

1- Less

0- NA

I do not think its possible to store texts into SAC measures and generate a matrix like you have shown in picture 1.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Sap analytics cloud - deleting version data using data action
entry.content:
@Eliram_Lugassi 

First all of do not use DATA()=0 for deletion, This can lead to significant explosion in 0 records and will affect your performance going forward.

If the purpose is to delete the records in Data Action advanced formula, Put the filters using MEMBERSET and then finally use DELETE() statement.

Now coming to the version part. You do not have to scope the version in DA formula as every DA advanced formula has an implicit TargetVersion parameter. So just scope other dimensions and version will be automatically asked when you run any DA. You can select the version for which you want to delete during execution of the DA.

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Auto Usage Decision for SAP PM Calibration Process

entry.content: @Freytag  You cannot import data from file. First you need to setup integration using Integration suite man aged gateway and then only you can import attributes from file.

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price changing after procurement of fr...
entry.content: Dear @raymond_giuseppi, The stock is owned by the company it is not supplier or customer stock, those materials are given free to the company for tests, after the tests, if the results are good the company places an order for the material with big quantities, and those free goods considered samples are comming from different suppliers, The company wants to follow those samples stocks without creating a seperate material code in the same time not impacting the moving average (But we must see them is stock in order to follow also the quality tests) . My question is also to check if their request is reasonnable

entry.title: Re: Urgent how DOCX(Xstring) is converted to PDF(Xstring)
entry.content:
Just asking: why do you need to convert to PDF? Normally you attach the Original documents to Invoice - as original. 

So if Original is DOCX why not attach DOCX?

Just wondering 🙂

entry.title: Re: No Handling Unit is there in warehouse task
entry.content:
Hello, 

Go to the config of Warehouse Process Type (Tcode = /SCWM/60000066) being used in the process and tick the indicator " Select HU w/o Storage Process" in Settings for Warehouse Requests section 

Regards,

entry.title: Connectivity Proxy Upgrade to 2.13.0
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have an issue while i'm trying to upgrade my connectivity proxy to version 2.13.0. This is what i get from the logs before it reaches ERROR state:

Starting Connectivity Proxy *                                                                                                                                                                            │
│ connectivity-proxy **************************************************************                                                                                                                                                                            │
│ connectivity-proxy 2025-02-28 09:17:40.308                      main             INFO K8sScpEnvironmentInterpreter:110 - Validated system property connectivity_proxy.tunnel.server.host=test.com                                  │
│ connectivity-proxy 2025-02-28 09:17:40.310                      main             INFO K8sScpEnvironmentInterpreter:110 - Validated system property connectivity_proxy.tunnel.server.port=443                                                                 │
│ connectivity-proxy 2025-02-28 09:17:40.310                      main             INFO K8sScpEnvironmentInterpreter:110 - Validated system property connectivity.agent.version=2.518.0                                                                        │
│ connectivity-proxy 2025-02-28 09:17:40.310                      main             INFO K8sScpEnvironmentInterpreter:110 - Validated system property connectivity_proxy.image.version=2.13.0                                                                   │
│ connectivity-proxy Exception in thread "main" java.lang.IllegalStateException: Missing or empty mandatory system property with key: connectivity_proxy.region_configurations.file                                                                            │
│ connectivity-proxy     at com.sap.core.connectivity.spi.environment.SystemEnvironmentInterpreter.getMandatoryProperty(SystemEnvironmentInterpreter.java:103)                                                                                                 │
│ connectivity-proxy     at com.sap.core.connectivity.spi.environment.SystemEnvironmentInterpreter.assertEnvironment(SystemEnvironmentInterpreter.java:79)                                                                                                     │
│ connectivity-proxy     at com.sap.core.connectivity.spi.environment.SystemEnvironmentInterpreter.<init>(SystemEnvironmentInterpreter.java:31)                                                                                                                │
│ connectivity-proxy     at com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.server.proxy.ConnectivityAgentEnvironmentInterpreter.<init>(ConnectivityAgentEnvironmentInterpreter.java:16)                                                                                      │
│ connectivity-proxy     at com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.k8s.scp.environment.AbstractK8sScpEnvironmentInterpreter.<init>(AbstractK8sScpEnvironmentInterpreter.java:52)                                                                                     │
│ connectivity-proxy     at com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.k8s.scp.environment.K8sScpEnvironmentInterpreter.<init>(K8sScpEnvironmentInterpreter.java:131)                                                                                                    │
│ connectivity-proxy     at com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.k8s.scp.environment.K8sScpEnvironmentInterpreter.<init>(K8sScpEnvironmentInterpreter.java:126)                                                                                                    │
│ connectivity-proxy     at com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.k8s.scp.environment.K8sScpEnvironmentInterpreter.<init>(K8sScpEnvironmentInterpreter.java:122)                                                                                                    │
│ connectivity-proxy     at com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.k8s.scp.K8sScpConnectivityAgent.<init>(K8sScpConnectivityAgent.java:67)                                                                                                                           │
│ connectivity-proxy     at com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.k8s.scp.K8sScpConnectivityAgent.<init>(K8sScpConnectivityAgent.java:6

entry.title: Re: Hi Experts i am trying to manipulate shelf life expir...

entry.title: Simulation Feature for Business Rules?
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to know if this feature really exist?

I found someone's blog here, stated that this feature exist.

https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/simulation-feature-sap-successfactors-chapter-12--5yjxc/

Seems I did not find any official document from help.sap.com with regards this specific feature.

Regards,
Reza

entry.title: Which one of "WITH or WITHOUT" FURTHER SECOND KEYS is the default for complete Table Types?
entry.content:
Hello,

Is there an error in the ABAP Keyword Documentation here about Table Type Keys, concerning the double use of "This addition expresses the default behavior in table types with a generic primary key":

I think that "WITHOUT further secondary keys" is the default for "complete primary key", not "generic", while a generic table type implies by default "WITH further secondary keys".

I did the test with the following program (which compiles):

 

REPORT.
" itab_1sk is compatible with TT_G (generic) but not with TT_C (complete).
" 
" itab_0sk is compatible with both TT_G and TT_C.
"
" So, a generic type is by default WITH FURTHER SECONDARY KEYS,
" and a complete type is by default WITHOUT FURTHER SECONDARY KEYS.

" With one secondary key
DATA itab_1sk TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapireturn WITH DEFAULT KEY WITH NON-UNIQUE SORTED KEY by_type COMPONENTS type.
" With zero secondary key
DATA itab_0sk TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapireturn WITH DEFAULT KEY.

" Generic
TYPES tt_g TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapireturn. 
" Complete
TYPES tt_c TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapireturn WITH DEFAULT KEY. 

PERFORM sr_g USING itab_0sk.
PERFORM sr_g USING itab_1sk.

PERFORM sr_c USING itab_0sk. " wouldn't compile with itab_1sk

FORM sr_g USING itab TYPE tt_g.
ENDFORM.

FORM sr_c USING itab TYPE tt_c.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: Re: Rules for OData datetime properties

Hi ,

May i know the exact syntax how to pass the input for time stamp ??Time stamp as a input in CDS View when i passed in API itr says expcted value is invalid key predicate .expected type is Edm.DateTimeOffset ?

entry.title: Re: TMS - Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CI/CPI) Content with Transport Management Service (TMS) a
entry.content:
Hi @gauravi_shukla ,

With regards to your comment talking about where exactly the Content Agent should be installed. I've also found this very confusing between this blog, your CAS blog (which shows it installed in the DEV/QA/PROD CPI subaccounts) and the SAP Help documentation, which is also not clear.

What is best practice? For example, we have subaccounts like this:

Subaccounts:

  • CPI DEV
  • CPI QA
  • CPI PROD
  • APPS DEV (Work Zone Standard, Mobile Services MDK)
  • APPS QA (Work Zone Standard, Mobile Services MDK)
  • APPS PROD (Work Zone Standard, Mobile Services MDK)
  • Central Admin Subaccount (Cloud Transport Management so far)

Where should the Content Agent be installed? I'm not someone who likes to duplicate things if we don't need to, so it was nice to see in this blog that CAS can be installed in a central subaccount (I had planned to install it in the same one where our Cloud Transport Management is installed). But I saw your comment saying that's incorrect and it should be installed in the CPI Dev subaccount.

Does that mean CAS should be installed in every Dev subaccount only, where we are transporting content? Or can it be installed in a central subaccount and that is a supported scenario?

What benefit do we get when CAS is installed in a Dev subaccount vs a central subaccount? Does the CPI Developer or Work Zone Standard administrator get additional features when transporting from CPI or WZS if CAS is installed in the Dev subaccount? Note that ideally, we don't want our developers to have to navigate to a different UI (such as the CAS UI) when transporting content from CPI or Work Zone Standard. We want our developers to stay inside the single tool they are used to as long as they are not losing any features.

And one last question seeing Tia Xu hasn't answered anyone since creating this Blog... Where he describes the destinations for deployment to the TEST_Node and PROD_Node, is it mandatory to use Basic authentication and an actual "user" who is a Space Developer in the CPI Test and Prod systems? Is it possible to instead use a Service Instance and OAuth client credentials on this destination instead? If so, what kind of Service Instance needs to be created?

Thanks,

Brendan

thank you, i wasn't sure about TargetVersion.

i changed it to DELETE()

As you said you know what debug is, you may also debug the standard to understand where your customizing is wrong / why the standard doesn't call the BAdI. It should be a matter of minutes.

Regards,

Shirsendu Roy

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Cloud Create Outgoing Invoices (FB70) - how to...
entry.content:
Thank you for your request, @MinhTriLe.

Note 2971277 - Long text is not displayed in Manage Journal Entries suggests using notes instead of long text. Please follow this suggestion. 

API Overview | Accounting Document - Read | SAP Business Accelerator Hub does provide for header and items' texts: DocumentItemText and AccountingDocumentHeaderText. Please try them out with your system.  

The Sandbox environment has a downtime until Thursday, March 06th 15:00 CET

If this helped you address the request, please mark accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: GL Account API to read accounts extended at company code leve
entry.content:
Please vote

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/342381

entry.title: Re: Extension field extension scenario in S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Thank you for your question, @ashisherplogic8.

For extending the sales documents you already got some response from the community.

I suppose you may want to learn where to find the business context on your own. I'd go with the APIs of the business process. You'd find the business processes in the Process Navigator - SAP for Me, e.g. for purchasing you might want to look up scope item J45, 

 

Among the accelerators you'd have the test script which shows which master data you might need, like product (a.k.a. material master). Further down in the description you'd see managing purchase orders among the key process flows. Thus, you might deduct the purchase order as one of the relevant business objects. 

In the SAP Business Accelerator Hub you can then look up by product 'S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition', APIs, All, search for 'Purchase Order', select the latest active OData API, here OData V4 API 'Purchase Order' Create Update Delete. On the Overview page of the API navigate to Extensibility tab. There you will find the Business Context for both header and items. 

If this answer helped you, please mark the response accordingly.

entry.title: Integration Suite: Storing SAP CPI Message Processing Logs in SAP HANA Cloud for Long Term Retention
entry.content:
Introduction:

In my previous blog, I have integrated Error Email Alerts with Message Processing Logs to notify recipients of failures. Now, we are taking it a step further by storing these logs in SAP HANA Cloud for better tracking and analysis. SAP Cloud Integration (SAP CPI) provides message processing logs, but these logs are only retained for 30 days. This limitation makes it difficult to analyze historical data, troubleshoot long-term issues, and meet compliance requirements.

To overcome this, we can store SAP CPI logs in SAP HANA Cloud, enabling long-term storage, advanced analysis. In this blog, we will walk through the step-by-step process of storing message processing logs in SAP HANA Cloud using JDBC integration.

If you haven't seen my blog on Automating Email Alerts for Failed Interfaces in SAP CPI, check it out here.

Why SAP HANA Cloud for Storing Logs?

Storing logs in HANA Cloud can provides several benefits:

  • Native SAP Cloud Solution - Easily integrates with SAP CPI and other SAP BTP services.
  • Long Term Storage - Retains logs beyond SAP CPI's 30 days limit.
  • Advanced Querying & Reporting - Use SQL based queries to analyze and visualize logs.
  • Integration with BI Tools- Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud or other reporting tools.
  • Secure & Scalable - Handle large volumes of logs with high availability.

Prerequisites:

  1. SAP HANA Cloud Subscription - Subscribe and create and instance in SAP Hana Cloud within your SAP BTP Account.
  2. Database Access - Permissions to create tables and manage data in your HANA Cloud Instance.
  3. SAP CPI OAuth Setup - Configure OAuth authentication in SAP CPI to access Message Processing Logs via OData API.

you can refer below 2 blogs to create SAP HANA Cloud instance and Configure JDBC Credentials in SAP CPI.

Part 1, Part 2

Once HANA Cloud instance got created

1. Create table in SAP HANA Cloud Instance.

Go to SQL Console

Select the Instance and enter username (DBADMIN) and password created (for first time login only)

Enter below SQL Query to create the Table

 

 

CREATE TABLE "SAPCPI_MessageProcessingLogs" (
 "MessageID" NVARCHAR(255),
 "CorrelationID" NVARCHAR(255),
 "ApplicationMessageId" NVARCHAR(255),
 "Status" NVARCHAR(50), -- New column added
 "IntegrationFlowName" NVARCHAR(255),
 "PackageName" NVARCHAR(255),
 "LogTime" TIMESTAMP,
 "ExceptionMessage" NCLOB,
 "MonitorLink" NVARCHAR(1000),
 "CreatedAt" TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

 

 

Once table created check once using select query as below screenshot.

2. Configure JDBC Material in SAP CPI

In Integration Suite, go to Monitor > Integrations and APIs > Manage Security > JDBC Material.

Configure the JDBC Data Source

3. Integration Flow Overview:

Our iFlow will follow these key steps:

  1. Retrieve Message Processing Logs – Fetch Interface logs from the MPL OData V2 API for a specified timeframe (e.g., since the last run). Also, Fetch the error message based on message Id using HTTP request.
  2. Build SQL Query – Construct an SQL query dynamically to insert records into HANA Cloud. HANA Cloud does not support inserting multiple records in a single INSERT INTO... VALUES(...) statement when using JDBC in SAP CPI. To work around this limitation, we use SELECT... FROM DUMMY with UNION ALL. 
  3. Insert Records  – Next step is to execute query using JDBC adapter. The query is passed as the payload to the JDBC receiver, which connects to SAP HANA Cloud.
  4. SAP_ApplicationID  – Once query executes, we will receive response back saying 'SQL statement returned OK, 578 row(s) affected'. we will store this text as SAP Application ID which will be visible in the logs for reference.

Download Integration flow from the Git : link

  • Download the Store SAP CPI Message Processing Logs in SAP HANA Cloud.zip from the Git.
  • Upload it in CPI
  • Go to configure and update below parameters.

Configure timer based on required frequency (e.g.,  every 60 min or daily once)

Receiver HANA_Cloud (JDBC):

JDBC Data Source AliasAlias name configured in the JDBC Data Sources (refer step 2)

Receiver CPI_ErrorMessage (HTTP):

AuthenticationBasic / OAuth2 Client Credentials
Credential NameName defined in the Security Material

Receiver CPI_MessageProcessingLogs (HCIOData):

AuthenticationBasic / OAuth2 Client Credentials
Credential NameName defined in the Security Material

More All Parameters:

LastRunDateTime Defaultlast run date time in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSS for the first run, later on value will be picked from local variable.
TableNameTable Name in the SAP HANA Cloud, this will be used in insert query.
Tenant URL

URL of CPI tenant by removing https:// <--->.com, refer below screenshot.

Click on save and deploy.

Result:

iFlow successfully processed:

Query generated dynamically based on the logs

Records (Logs) inserted in the HANA database:

Use Cases for Stored Logs in SAP HANA Cloud:

  • Retain Logs beyond SAP CPI's default retention period.
  • Provide access to business partners for reviewing errors.
  • Generate custom reports for business and IT teams.
  • Perform advanced analytics on interface performance and errors.
  • Integrate with visualization tools like SAP Analytics Cloud for insights.
  • Retrieve the data based on query parameters with JDBC for faster response than MPL OData API.

Considerations and Limitations:

This solution does not use a primary key in the table, which means duplicate records may be inserted if the same message is processed multiple times, so deduplication logic might be needed. Additionally, error handling for JDBC failures is not implemented, meaning if the insert query fails, logs will not be stored, and there is no retry mechanism. This blog provides a basic overview, and you can enhance the iFlow based on your requirements by adding filtering, error handling, or query optimizations. Also, I am not an SQL expert, so I have used basic SQL statements, and there may be better ways to optimize the queries.

Conclusion:

In this blog, we explored how to store SAP CPI logs in SAP HANA Cloud for long-term retention, easy querying, and analytics. This approach helps overcome SAP CPI’s log retention limits and provides business users with direct access to error logs without relying on CPI monitoring. By leveraging SAP HANA Cloud’s native capabilities within SAP BTP, organizations can enhance visibility into their integration processes, improve troubleshooting, and gain deeper insights.

Let me know your thoughts or if you have any questions drop a comment below!

Happy Integrating!

entry.title: Re: Get All cost centers based on date&time creation thro...
entry.content:
Thank you for your request, @amer_alsharif.

For profit centers, it does look like the API has a method that might list all of them, without the need to give any further inputs. See the blue marked GET /ProfitCenter entry on the left-hand side and the respective (lack of specific required) parameters.

For Cost Centers, the API  | Cost Center | SAP Business Accelerator Hub also has a similar GET /A_CostCenter_2 method which should yield all of the cost centers without any further input parameters needed.

 

If this response solved your business requirements, please mark it accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Steps to Create a Replication Flow in SAP Datasphere for an Existing CDS View in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Akshat,

thanks for your blog providing also the settings in the cloud connector, very appreciated.

Did you choose DPAgent or Cloud Connector when creating the connection in DSP in the section "Remote Tables"?

May I kindly ask you to add the information which S/4HANA version is needed to be able to see "CDS_EXTRACTION" and "SLT" in DSP?

It explain why the mentioned entries do not appear in DSP and as a result of this why CDS view from S/4 are not available.

entry.title: Re: Condition Contract - Defaulting BVB
entry.content:
Hi Michele,

SAP does not support multiple default values for a field within a field combination. The relevant coding is in method  FILL_FROM_CONTRACT_DATA in class CL_WB2_BVB_SERVICES. To my knowledge no BAdI exists that can set the default values.

I assume that it is possible to use different field values for different field combinations. This requires that you create as many field combinations for the same field as you need different values. In addition you have to assign these field combinations to the same "Field Combination Group for Business Volume Selection" within IMG item "Define and Configure Sets of Field Combinations".

"Reference Condition Contract for Bus Vol Selection Criteria" might be also an alternative. This requires a field combination that contains the field BVB_LIST. From the documentation for the corresponding data element WB2_BVB_LIST:
Definition
Condition Contract that serves as reference for business volume selection criteria.
Use
Business volume selection criteria specified for the reference condition contract are used for the business volume determination of the actual condition contract.
The usage of reference condition contracts for business volume selection criteria facilitates the condition contract maintenance when identical selection criteria shall be used in several condition contracts.

See also 2401084 - Selection criteria for other contracts are not taken into account - SAP for Me and IMG Item "Specify Relevant CC Types for Field Combinations Representing a BVB List".

Regards
Michael

entry.title: Re: Develop Diverse SuccessFactors integration
entry.content:
Hi @Chris_Cantwell ,

Develop Diverse is not widely discussed in the SuccessFactors ecosystem, its API integration with SuccessFactors Recruiting seems like a promising tool for improving diversity and inclusion in hiring processes. The main challenges often lie in ensuring proper integration, smooth data flow, and maintaining a consistent experience across systems.

Diversity Tracking: One of the main benefits that customers have found is that the tool enables more robust tracking and reporting of diversity data within the recruitment process. This helps with:

Measuring D&I progress: It offers insights on where the company stands in terms of diversity and inclusion goals and allows you to track initiatives over time.

Improved Hiring: With the integration, users can leverage SuccessFactors Recruiting and Career Site Builder alongside Develop Diverse to offer insights on candidate diversity throughout the hiring journey.

Enhanced Reporting: The integration can improve reporting features, making it easier for HR teams to generate diversity-focused reports that help in decision-making.

How the Integration Works

  • API Connectivity: Develop Diverse provides an API that facilitates data exchange with SuccessFactors. This enables companies to bring diversity data from Develop Diverse into SuccessFactors Recruiting for analysis and reporting. For example:
  • Candidate Data: Demographic information, hiring data, and diversity-related attributes of candidates can be pulled into SuccessFactors.
  • Tracking & Analytics: Integration allows for more granular tracking of diversity metrics within the SuccessFactors Recruiting and Onboarding modules.
  • Integration Points: This typically involves integrating with modules like:
    • Recruiting: To assess candidate pool diversity, track D&I goals, and improve inclusive hiring.
    • Onboarding: To integrate diversity-related metrics into onboarding processes.

Hope this helps!!

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: Community Member Spotlight: Roberta Fernandes - February 2025 Edition
entry.content: Congratulations and thank you for the support.

entry.title: Creation of CAPM Project
entry.content:
Steps to Create and Deploy a CAPM Project in SAP BTP

Access SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)

  • Log in to your SAP BTP Cockpit.

  • Navigate to Services > SAP Business Application Studio.

  • Click Go to Application to open BAS.

Create a Dev Space for CAP Development

  • In BAS, create a new dev space:

    • Choose the SAP Cloud Business Application template.

    • Ensure the CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) extension is included.

    • Provide a name for your dev space.

  • Click Create Dev Space and wait for it to initialize.

Open the Dev Space

  • Once the dev space is ready, click on its name to open it.

  • The BAS interface will load, and you’ll see the workspace.

there are two primary approaches to create a CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) project in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS):

  1. Using the Command Line Interface (CLI)

  2. Using the CAP Project Wizard (GUI-Based)

Below, I’ll explain both approaches in detail so you can choose the one that best suits your workflow.

1. Using the Command Line Interface (CLI)

This approach involves using the CAP CLI (Command Line Interface) to create and manage your CAP project. It’s a quick and flexible method, especially if you’re comfortable working in a terminal.

Steps to Create a CAP Project Using CLI:

Open the Terminal in BAS:

  • Open your dev space in SAP Business Application Studio.

  • Open the terminal.

  • Execute the command cds init my-cap-project (Initialize the CAPM Project).
  • cd my-cap-project (Navigate to the Project folder)

Using the CAP Project Wizard (GUI-Based)

This approach uses the CAP Project Wizard in SAP Business Application Studio. It’s a more visual and guided method, ideal for beginners or those who prefer a GUI-based workflow.

Steps to Create a CAP Project Using the Wizard:

  1. Open SAP Business Application Studio:

    • Log in to your SAP BTP account and open BAS.

    • Open your dev space.

  2. Open the CAP Project Wizard:

    • In the BAS interface, go to the Welcome Page (if it doesn’t open automatically, you can access it via the Help menu).

    • Under the Start from a template section, click Start CAP Application.

  3. Configure the Project:

    • The CAP Project Wizard will open.

    • Provide the following details:

      • Project Name: Enter a name for your project 

      • Template: Choose a template ( Basic CAP Project).

      • Add Sample Data: Optionally, include sample data to get started quickly.

    • Click Finish.

  4. Files and Folders Created in a CAP Project

    1. package.json

      • Defines the project metadata, dependencies, and scripts for building and running the application.

    2. cds Folder

      • Contains configuration files for the CAP server, such as cdsrc.json (custom configurations) and package.json (CAP-specific dependencies).

    3. db/ Folder

      • Contains data model files (e.g., data-model.cds) where you define your database entities and relationships.

    4. srv/ Folder

      • Contains service definition files (e.g., cat-service.cds) where you define the services exposing the data models.

    5. app/ Folder (Optional)

      • Contains UI components (e.g., Fiori elements or freestyle SAPUI5 apps) if you selected an Fiori template in the Wizard.

    6. mta.yaml (After Running cds add mta)

      • Defines the Multi-Target Application (MTA) configuration for deploying the project to SAP BTP.

    7. README.md

      • A markdown file providing basic instructions and information about the project.

    8. .gitignore

      • Specifies files and folders to be ignored by Git (e.g., node_modules/, mta_archives/).

    9. node_modules/ (After Running npm install)

      • Contains all the installed npm dependencies for the project.

    10. gen/ Folder (Generated Files)

      • Contains generated files during the build process, such as CSN (Core Schema Notation) files.

  • Add Data Models and Services:

    • Create a data model in the db/ folder (e.g., data-model.cds).

    • Define your entities and associations.

    • Create a service definition in the srv/ folder (e.g., cat-service.cds).

    • Link the service to the data model.

 

Once the project is created, Need to add model and services, after that we need to execute the project in Development using cds watch command. The cds watch command is a powerful feature of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) that allows you to run and monitor your CAP project in development mode. It automatically reloads your application whenever you make changes to your code, making it ideal for rapid development and testing.

What Does cds watch Do?

  1. Starts the CAP Server:

    • Launches the CAP server, which serves your data models and services.

    • By default, it runs on http://localhost:4004.

  2. Auto-Reload:

    • Watches for changes in your project files (e.g., .cds, .js, .json).

    • Automatically restarts the server when changes are detected.

  3. Logs and Debugging:

    • Provides real-time logs in the terminal, making it easy to debug issues.

  4. Service Endpoints:

    • Exposes your service endpoints (e.g., OData services) for testing.

How to Use cds watch:

open terminal: execute cds watch.

Once the server starts, you’ll see output like this:

[cds] - serving CatalogService at /catalog - impl: cat-service.js
[cds] - server listening on http://localhost:4004

Open your browser and navigate to:

  • Service Endpoint: http://localhost:4004/catalog/Products

  • Fiori Preview: http://localhost:4004/$fiori-preview (if you have a UI).

Stopping cds watch

  • To stop the server, press Ctrl + C in the terminal.

entry.title: Using HANA Cloud’s "VECTOR_EMBEDDING" Function in CAP
entry.content:
Introduction

In Q4 2024, SAP HANA Cloud introduced a feature that enables embedding generation. Previously, embeddings were obtained using SAP AI Core or external LLMs, but now they can be generated directly within SAP HANA Cloud. In this blog, I will demonstrate how to use this feature in CAP. 

 

Prerequisite

Enable Natural Language Processing (NLP) in the SAP HANA Cloud settings.

 

Basic Syntax

The following syntax is used to retrieve an embedding:

 

SELECT VECTOR_EMBEDDING('Hello world!','DOCUMENT','SAP_NEB.20240715') FROM DUMMY;

<text> : The text for which an embedding is created.

<text-type>: The type of the text. Must be either 'DOCUMENT' or 'QUERY'.

<model-and-version>: The name and the version of the model that is used to create the embedding.  

VECTOR_EMBEDDING Function (Vector) | Help Portal

 

Currently, only one model (SAP_NEB.20240715) is available, which generates an embedding with 768 dimensions.

 

CAP Application

I will introduce the sample CAP application I created. The code is available on GitHub.

DB schema

 

namespace cap.embedding.function;
using { cuid, managed } from '@sap/cds/common';

entity Dummy {
  key id : Integer;
}

entity Notes: cuid, managed {
  note: LargeString;
  embedding: Vector(768);
}

 

Key Points in Data Definitions

Use of the "Dummy" table

If you want to retrieve an embedding for a text that does not reside in a database table, you may want to use a syntax as below:

 

SELECT VECTOR_EMBEDDING('Hello world!','DOCUMENT','SAP_NEB.20240715') FROM DUMMY;

 

However, since CAP does not have a  DUMMY table, I created one to ensure it always contains exactly one record. I will show how to use this table in the next section.

Embedding Column Dimensions

The embedding column's dimension should be set based on to the model you use. Since we are using SAP_NEB.20240715, the dimension is 768. 

 

Service definition

 

using { cap.embedding.function as db } from '../db/schema';

service EmbeddingStorageService {
    entity Notes as projection on db.Notes excluding {
        embedding
    };

    action addNotes(notes: array of Notes) returns String;
    action deleteNotes() returns String;
    function similaritySearch(searchWord: String) returns array of Notes;
}

 

This service exposes the Notes entity and defines two actions for bulk inserting and deleting notes. Finally, the similaritySearch function returns the top three relevant notes based on the input searchWord.

 

Event Handlers

Storing an Embedding to the Database

 

   this.before(['CREATE', 'UPDATE'], 'Notes', async (req) => {
      const embedding = await this.getEmbedding(req.data.note)
      req.data.embedding = JSON.stringify(embedding)
    })

  async getEmbedding(text) {
    const db = await cds.connect.to('db')
    const { Dummy } = db.entities

    // get embedding
    const { embedding } = await SELECT.one.from(Dummy).where({ id: 1 })
                              .columns `vector_embedding(${text}, 'DOCUMENT', 'SAP_NEB.20240715') as embedding`
    return embedding
  }

 

The getEmbedding function retrieves an embedding using vector_embedding function. Since the result is an array, it must be converted to a string before storing it into the database.

 

Performing a Similarity Search

 

    this.on('similaritySearch', async (req) => {
      const searchWord = req.data.searchWord
      const embedding = await this.getEmbedding(searchWord)
      
      const db = await cds.connect.to('db')
      const { Notes } = db.entities

      // retrieve relevant notes
      const notes = await SELECT.from(Notes)
                                .columns('ID', 'note')
                                .limit(3)
                                // .where`cosine_similarity(embedding, to_real_vector(${JSON.stringify(embedding)})) > 0.7`
                                .orderBy`cosine_similarity(embedding, to_real_vector(${JSON.stringify(embedding)})) desc`
    
      return notes                    
    })

 

This function first retrieves an embedding for a given search word, then executes a similarity search to get the top three relevant notes.

 

Testing

The following notes are stored in the database.

 

{
  "@odata.context": "$metadata#Notes",
  "value": [
    {
      "ID": "02e197c9-4f06-4abf-b314-37d89226dfb0",
      "note": "Remember to buy milk, eggs, and bread on the way home."
    },
    {
      "ID": "31ccffee-e186-4ec8-90c2-cfdee5e9a77f",
      "note": "AWS EC2 instance needs to be restarted after applying updates."
    },
    {
      "ID": "8a78b77d-7210-465d-861f-f2d4ff249371",
      "note": "Read 'The Lean Startup' by Eric Ries for innovation insights."
    },
    {
      "ID": "9841088a-d5b8-44ac-8841-e7f3909d1cc8",
      "note": "Meeting with John on Monday at 10 AM about project roadmap."
    },
    {
      "ID": "d65a1f36-ede1-4808-9bfc-a38f1a7dc888",
      "note": "Flight to San Francisco departs at 3:45 PM on April 12th."
    }
  ]
}

 

Now, let's perform a similarity search using the search word "shopping list".

 

### Similarity Search
@searchWord = shopping list
GET {{server}}/odata/v4/embedding-storage/similaritySearch(searchWord='{{searchWord}}')

 

The following three records are returned, with the most relevant appearing at the top.

 

{
  "@odata.context": "$metadata#Notes",
  "value": [
    {
      "ID": "02e197c9-4f06-4abf-b314-37d89226dfb0",
      "note": "Remember to buy milk, eggs, and bread on the way home."
    },
    {
      "ID": "9841088a-d5b8-44ac-8841-e7f3909d1cc8",
      "note": "Meeting with John on Monday at 10 AM about project roadmap."
    },
    {
      "ID": "d65a1f36-ede1-4808-9bfc-a38f1a7dc888",
      "note": "Flight to San Francisco departs at 3:45 PM on April 12th."
    }
  ]
}

 

 

Conclusion

With the VECTOR_EMBEDDING function, you can retrieve embeddings without needing SAP AI Core or external LLM services. However, in real-life RAG scenarios, LLMs are still required. Consider this a starting point and extend it to fit your specific use case.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2022 System Conversion Steps & Details – How to be prepared?
entry.content: Very useful content. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Configuring the SuccessFactors Employee Central > Advance Types and Deductions
entry.content:
Neatly presented, I have one question. How the system will behave if an employee goes on unpaid leave?

 

Regards

Siddharth

entry.title: Cash Mgm: how exclude FOC Sales Order flow
entry.content:
Hi Gurus, 

I'd like to know how to avoid free of charge sales orders in FQM_FLOW table.

I mean,  some SD scenarios generate sales order that will not be posted in Accounts Receivable, only taxes in GL Account, like: sample, bonus, plant transference  etc. 

These scenarios should not be displayed in Cashflow because it's not a liquidity forecast. 

I've  already reviewed all FSCM customizing and search a sap notes.

Could you please help me.

Thanks in advance. 

BR

Att

RL
